== Notes for [[Item:Q52568|Q52568]] ==

{geni:about_me} `HE BECAME A FAMOUS AND CHAKRAVARTI (IDEAL UNIVERSAL RULER) KING. HE DEFEATED MOST OF THE OTHER KINGS OF HIS TIME. HE MARRIED BINDUMATI, A DAUGHTER OF THE CHANDRAVANSHI KING..
<p>http://www.gloriousindia.com/history/dynasties/ikshvaku/index.html</p>
<p>http://www.gloriousindia.com/history/dynasties/ikshvaku/mandhata.html Mandhata's wife is Chaitrarathi, who is also called as Bindumati, the daughter of king Shasha-Bindu, </p>
<p>[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mandhatri Mandhatri or Mandhata (Sanskrit: मान्धातृ, Māndhātṛ)], in Hindu mythology, was an Ikshvaku dynasty king and son of Yuvanashva.... He married Bindumati Chaitrarathi, daughter of Yadava king Shashabindu and granddaughter of Chitraratha.[3] According to the Puranas, he had three sons, Purukutsa, Ambarisha and Muchukunda.[4] According to the Mahabharata, he was a son of the Suryavansha king Yuvanshva... Mandhata was also known as Yauvanashwin (Son of Yuvanashwa) and Trassadasyu (One who was feared by the wicked).... Mandhata married the Chandravanshi princess, Bindumati, the daughter of Shashabindu, King of the Yadavas. The couple had three sons and fifty daughters. Mandhata's daughters fell in love with the handsome ascetic Saubhari and married him. Mandhata's eldest son, Susandhi succeeded his father.

[[Category:GEDCOM Notes Pages]][[Category:Pages]][[Category:Pages]]
